Project Context
Background & Policy Mandate

Jiande City has pursued systematic wastewater improvement since 2014 under Zhejiang's "Five-Water Co-governance" programme. This project responds to the provincial Strong Foundation, Double Standard Action Plan (2021–2025), which mandates ≥95% administrative village coverage and ≥95% effluent compliance by 2025.

Wanxing Village (Wanxing Village) is used as the case-study representative. The village spans 6.3 km², with 1,762 residents across 4 natural hamlets. Before this project, only 224 of 604 registered households were connected. The Wushiqiao natural hamlet — the focus area — had no sewer network; wastewater discharged directly to natural watercourses.

Geographic Scope
Project Location Map
10 Villages Covered — Datong Town
Wanxing Village ★
Design & Field Work Process
Six-Week Field Investigation to Construction Supervision
01
Field Survey

Six weeks of on-site investigation: terrain and topographic survey, elevation profiling across Wushiqiao hamlet (101–124 m range), drainage pattern mapping.

02
Household Visits

Door-to-door interviews with 101 new-connection households. Assessed existing septic tanks, kitchen facilities, and wastewater generation. Site photos documented throughout.

03
Preliminary Design

Authored full design report: network layout, longitudinal profiles, A²O treatment system, LID integration, structural drawings, energy and cost analysis.

04
Construction Supervision

Provided on-site design support during construction — resolving drawing queries, clarifying specifications, and ensuring pipe installation aligned with design intent.

Key Engineering Drawings & Field Work
Technical Deliverables · Wushiqiao Natural Hamlet, Wanxing Village
Sewerage Layout Plan
Plan-view pipe network: main, branch, and household connection pipes; manhole positions; gravity-flow routing along existing roads to minimise excavation.
Longitudinal Profile of Sewer Network
Elevation cross-section: pipe invert levels, cover depths, gradient, and manhole spacing — from highest household (124.15 m) to treatment terminal (101 m).
Low-Impact Development (LID)
Integrated stormwater management: rain-pollutant separation, vegetated buffer zones, soil conservation planting, and runoff retention measures.
Field Work Documentation
